rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Known correct Claude Code facts (Plan mode = Shift+Tab cycle / /plan).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Lecture names plan mode as Shift+Tab cycle with status bar indicator — re-verified against code.claude.com/docs/en/permission-modes.md via WebFetch this audit (2026-05-22).

All platform claims (permission state, Shift+Tab, read-only behavior with reads/shell-explore/write-plan but no edit/execute until approval, descriptive plan-file naming) match shipped reality. Source URL still alive 2026-05-22.

Plan-file descriptive-name claim (`migrate-auth-hash-calm-otter.md`) not explicit in canonical docs (docs describe session auto-naming on approval), but practitioner-observed; sibling exercise push-back-on-the-plan.md uses the identical example consistently. Within §5 carve-out.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Sessions are the unit of work — 'start a new session,' not 'open Claude Code.'
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Opens with 'Start a new Claude Code session at your repo root' — correct register. Body uses 'session' consistently ('your own session right now,' 'other sessions could be making progress').

Maintainer block carries URL + [practitioner direct] label + verified-date stamp 2026-04-22. No body-prose label leak. Three-things / three-pressures framing explicitly scoped as exercise-scaffold, no citation needed.
Source verified 2026-04-22; current date 2026-05-22 — within 6-month window.
No statistics in lecture.
Single fenced prompt contains plain text only.
Lead-in present and mirrors prompt opening: 'Ask Claude to describe what changed in its behaviour when plan mode turned on.' Verb + noun-phrase parallel — also satisfies 2026-05-18 mirroring rule.

No skills/tools/paths invoked that would need install.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body — technical angle.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Lecture is primer-before-exercise (per maintainer-block note); forcing functions live in the M2 push-back exercise. Lecture's optional 30-second move is a fenced prompt (legal — fenced is always permitted).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Rule that does not force is worse than no rule.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Lecture content, not a process rule.
Lecture content, not orchestrator behavior.
Lecture, no verification machinery built.
Lecture does not deal with context-loading mechanics.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Plan mode in Claude Code is a permission state, not a feature.
You press Shift+Tab until the status bar reads plan.
The agent is now read-only. It reads files, runs shell commands to explore, writes a plan file, but it can't edit or execute until you approve.
The plan file has a descriptive name now (migrate-auth-hash-calm-otter.md, not random words).

citations_checked.claim: Plan mode (Anthropic Claude Code) — capability reference, current as of 2026-04-22; code.claude.com/docs/en/permission-modes.md [practitioner direct].
